text:
What's something you wish you knew about UNCC before attending?
Archived post. New comments cannot be posted and votes cannot be cast.
As annoying as things can be, we have WAY more opportunities and resources than some other schools. If truly is a great value for the education
It REALLY is insane
I feel like this will likely take a negative turn, but I'll offer something positive.
I always enjoyed how it felt like Charlotte was full of opportunity to anyone who was willing to seize it.

enjoyed how it felt like Charlotte was full of opportunity to anyone who was willing to seize it. Speaking as someone who went to a wealthy, small, elite, private school for grad school, I saw an environment where outcomes were destined from Day One when students stepped foot on campus. Daddy's golfing buddies had already set up Little Johnny with an elite internship, regardless of how he stacked up against his peers.
Charlotte never felt like that.

internship, regardless of how he stacked up against his peers.
Charlotte never felt like that. Charlotte felt like if you came here, worked hard, and did well, the sky was the limit. Conversely, things weren't just going to be handed to you just because of who you were or where you came from.
In a very naive sense of what the American Dream is, Charlotte felt like it fit that narrative that you can come here and truly make your experience whatever you dream it to be.

that narrative that you can come here and truly make your experience whatever you dream it to be. Nobody told me that was what this place was like, and I'm forever thankful for it.
Earth Sciences
I came in for grad school so a bit different perspective. I wish I knew how cohesive the departments are and how interdisciplinary the faculty are. At my undergrad, professors only knew the ones in their department. Here, they know people all over.

my undergrad, professors only knew the ones in their department. Here, they know people all over. Which makes it a great networking source & give other perspectives.
Also the hills. Who builds a campus on a giant hill.
OneIT Staff
Also the hills. Who builds a campus on a giant hill.
All an elaborate plan to make sure we get enough exercise.
Been to Appalachian State? Nothing but hills.
i always like to say that at app state, they are built nearby mountains.

State? Nothing but hills.
i always like to say that at app state, they are built nearby mountains. but try walking up craver road one time and you will soon realise that charlotte is built ON a mountain
My two main schools I want to go to and applied for are App State and UNC Charlotte lol. Guess I like hills!
Dont be so anxious. Get out there and have some fun. There's a lot of campus events and it'll surprise you how friendly the general student body is.

There's a lot of campus events and it'll surprise you how friendly the general student body is. I've went to two diff colleges prior and UNCC is by far the most welcoming personally. Also, get here early if you commute. Parkings rough.
The fact that people are very friendly and open here. Most of my friends are outside of my major which isn't something I can guarantee for students in other colleges.
The hills
I wish I knew that uncc was the greatest school of all time
Alumnus

in other colleges.
The hills
I wish I knew that uncc was the greatest school of all time
Alumnus
You may only cross Craver Road when the walk sign is on.
Not before. Not after.
That four other schools have NC promise with capped tuition for equally competitive degrees 😭
What degrees?
the history.

have NC promise with capped tuition for equally competitive degrees 😭
What degrees?
the history. i didn't read ken sanford's book until this semester and now i'm gonna graduate in december with an incredibly high appreciation for what the school has become when that could have happened years ago lol
The history of our school is genuinely I'm proud of, especially back in the Bonnie Cone days.
UNCC has a lot of resources, but doesn't tell you about them.

back in the Bonnie Cone days.
UNCC has a lot of resources, but doesn't tell you about them. There is a specific major for just about anything you'd want to do with a psychology degree besides strict psychology.
Because many people don't know this, the psychology major is bloated with something like 1600 students.
Those 1600 students make getting help from advisors extremely difficult ( I haven't even spoken to mine in about two years ).

help from advisors extremely difficult ( I haven't even spoken to mine in about two years ).
The department has little to no connection to resources outside the campus, and on campus, not every professor has a research lab, and if they do, labs only take a small handful of people at a time. And what do psych grad schools want to see? That you have valid research experience.
Additionally, grad schools want to see that you typically have at least three letters of recommendation.

grad schools want to see that you typically have at least three letters of recommendation. It is extra helpful if some of those letters come from professionals in your field. This is very hard to do when there are some 1600 students who might pass by them and also seek them. Even harder yet, when a significant portion of your teachers teach online classes. Last I heard, the psychology department was even pushing for more online classes.

online classes. Last I heard, the psychology department was even pushing for more online classes.
This is my final semester, and I have only secured some from Psyche just now.
So, very rough being a psychology major. To be fair, even with all this, I still probably would have gone to UNCC due to its modest cost and convenience, but it would have been good to know, and I could have started doing prep-work with all this in mind much sooner.
The grave in the garden

I could have started doing prep-work with all this in mind much sooner.
The grave in the garden
And don't forget the cemetery near the baseball stadium .
What? The botanical garden down the hill from the greenhouse?
Yeah! Bonnie Cone's grave is in the garden, near Polly's sensory area.
Wow, how have I never noticed that. I love that garden.

Chapel Hill or UNCC For Computer Science? Please Help
Academic
As the title suggests I am struggling to determine whether or not I should go to UNC Chapel Hill or UNCC. I have been dead set on chapel hill for the longest time but I recently took a tour to UNCC and wow I was impressed and feel myself shifting towards it. I even got to explore around the college of computing (I am pursuing computer science, particularly cybersecurity) and I pretty much fell in love with the program there.

science, particularly cybersecurity) and I pretty much fell in love with the program there. From what I see online, Chapel Hill's program is "better" but I feel myself gravitating towards Charlotte's program. I'm also concerned about the large amount of commuting on campus, and when I was there I noticed a lack of people. So I was wondering how that affects the campus life? Anybody got any wisdom that would help?
Edit: I got into the UNCC honors college, if that helps

Anybody got any wisdom that would help?
Edit: I got into the UNCC honors college, if that helps
Edit 2: I ended up going for Chapel Hill. I did more research into their program and it was beyond amazing!

Collapse video player
0:00 / 0:00
I went to UNCC for CS.

PRs moving with Codex. Try Codex for Free.
Collapse video player
0:00 / 0:00
I went to UNCC for CS. I work at a major bank with other UNCC grads and many Chapel Hill grads~ so, in terms of real life, there isn't much of a difference. Personal opinion- Chapel Hill is very well known, so the prestige is there. One thing I appreciated about UNCC is that it has the distinction of CCI as well as the "concentrations".

I appreciated about UNCC is that it has the distinction of CCI as well as the "concentrations". What I had heard about the UNC Chapel Hill program is that many people struggled to get credits for higher level classes due to how limited the seats/times were. This may not be the case now (class of 2023) As far as campus life goes, I believe UNCC is more of a commuter school; I participated in a handful of student organizations but I never lived on campus or in student housing.

in a handful of student organizations but I never lived on campus or in student housing.
Another thing that may help you make a decision is career prospects. Charlotte has a lot of tech and banking opportunities. Raleigh/Durham area is more of health and research focus (with some tech companies scattered around)
Either school will get you what you need- I also think being in North Carolina is already a plus for this degree, with many companies setting sites on building offices or headquarters.

a plus for this degree, with many companies setting sites on building offices or headquarters.
UnoriginalName432
Thank you so much! I got into the honors college at UNCC, so the priority registration that comes with is a huge part of my consideration because I don't think I'll be lucky enough to get all the classes I want.

part of my consideration because I don't think I'll be lucky enough to get all the classes I want.
I would like to also mention that back when I was attending, I was on the board of ACM-W; we would frequently host interview prep/practices, go to hackathons (funny enough, Pearl Hacks at Chapel Hill 🤣), tour company offices, and host "Tech Talks", being active in a student org will help get you plenty of exposure and make connections.

Talks", being active in a student org will help get you plenty of exposure and make connections. Many companies are keen on hiring talent from UNCC and will donate money to these student organizations. I can only assume that this also happens at Chapel Hill :) so maybe look into the student organizations and how active they are. I also had good experiences with the professors- there are a handful that will recommend you for internships/full time positions if you do well in class.

are a handful that will recommend you for internships/full time positions if you do well in class. Looking back on my personal experience, now I want to be bias, I think you should choose UNCC haha :)
Still picking computer science? Have you discussed that with anyone? Are you sure?
UnoriginalName432
That's why I'm pursuing cybersecurity. I think there's growth and potential in it compared to the rest of the industry. Is this copium? Maybe but I don't have anything I wanna do
This is copium.

rest of the industry. Is this copium? Maybe but I don't have anything I wanna do
This is copium. I go to career fairs to represent my job often. We are tech adjacent and we get completely mobbed by people asking for software engineers and data science roles. Computer science majors even fly in from out of state to go to job fairs. I think it was considered a "get rich quick" major for a long time which flooded the market. I would definitely explore other interests.
Not at ALL.

for a long time which flooded the market. I would definitely explore other interests.
Not at ALL. alum here, did undergrad and my masters in cybersecurity. I 11/10 recommend pursuing cybersecurity but shift focus to AI based cybersecurity: attacks, threats, vulnerabilities. Definitely not a get rich quick given the economic conditions but still possible. With persistence/grit, you can get anything done.
I also recommend getting into pen testing but with AI..

you can get anything done.
I also recommend getting into pen testing but with AI.. that's the future, at least the next 10-15 years. Learn this and you will have a job or at least internship while in school. DM me if you have any questions.
and also, biased or not, even logically speaking, UNCC > state/unc for CS/cyber
Lots of cybersecurity opportunities in Charlotte, just something to think about. I got a homie working cybersecurity for one of those big banks in CLT

something to think about. I got a homie working cybersecurity for one of those big banks in CLT
A lot like another comment on this post. I went to UNCC plus I work at a major bank here in Charlotte and also work with a lot of grads from UNC. If you genuinely are interested in cs Charlotte has a great CS program with pretty good opportunity for a career given its location.
This is my completely anecdotal experience that I am sharing.

for a career given its location.
This is my completely anecdotal experience that I am sharing. I am a uncc CS 2025 grad and work with a chapel hill CS 2025 grad. We discussed salaries and I know I started off higher than him and still make more than him, we worked together as interns summer 2024 and I was offered an extended internship while I was still in college, which resulted in a full time offer, and he was not offered any of this.

I was still in college, which resulted in a full time offer, and he was not offered any of this. He's back with us now because he applied after he graduated. He is super bright so it's not at all that he wasn't good enough or anything like that. I honestly don't know why I was chosen over any of the interns in my group that summer LOL.

that. I honestly don't know why I was chosen over any of the interns in my group that summer LOL.
Both my coworkers went to prestigious schools, Georgia Tech and Chapel Hill, and my boss did as well, and my boss has pointed out how she is glad she picked people who went to such great schools because they're so competent.

out how she is glad she picked people who went to such great schools because they're so competent. It makes me feel awkward because she's shat on uncc but always includes me in like "and you're super smart too of course!" It's weird and idk how to feel about it, I still think about whether or not it was a mistake to choose uncc, but then again I am currently at the same exact place as my coworkers so who knows
Seems like a boss issue.

again I am currently at the same exact place as my coworkers so who knows
Seems like a boss issue. Weird to shit on an R1 school that's top 75 in public universities in the nation with a stout CS program. Sure we're not Georgia Tech but it's still a very strong program they normally is pretty well respected
I completely agree with you! I always found it really rude.

they normally is pretty well respected
I completely agree with you! I always found it really rude. I went to a game with my job once too and someones +1 did the same thing after I mentioned having gone there, the perception of the school amongst older people doesn't seem to be good
sounds like boss is insecure, not a mistake to attend a school that... well has a dedicated school for computer science rather than shoving it in engineering schools/departments.

a dedicated school for computer science rather than shoving it in engineering schools/departments. Given how rapidly AI is progressing, it is easier for a school with a dedicated CS program/school to integrate these new concepts rather than adding them as a class in the eng dept.
NC State, ideally. Best engineering programs in the state by far. If it isn't too late to apply there, it is definitely worth looking into.

in the state by far. If it isn't too late to apply there, it is definitely worth looking into. I went there for engineering and it really was a fantastic program through and through.
To your question, though: I'd definitely put UNCC above chapel hill for any type of engineering.

your question, though: I'd definitely put UNCC above chapel hill for any type of engineering. Iirc, Chapel hill only has biomedical engineering (which is actually partnered with NC State and is part of their program, too, so even that one really isn't directly located within the CH's programs) and computer science in terms of the engineering degrees they offer. While it is a well-known school, the fact that they don't really focus on engineering would give me pause.

is a well-known school, the fact that they don't really focus on engineering would give me pause. UNCC, on the other hand, has plenty of engineering options and is still a great school. So based on those factors, I'd say UNCC.
Then there's also the possibility that you'll get in a computer science program and realize you like some other type of engineering discipline better. In that case, UNCC offers tons of other options that you can easily jump to; Chapel hill does not.

In that case, UNCC offers tons of other options that you can easily jump to; Chapel hill does not. Just something else to consider.
Good luck to ya and get ready for the rubber to hit the road, my friend!

UNCC hands down. CCI has an awesome program and opportunities within the technology sector from alumni.

hands down. CCI has an awesome program and opportunities within the technology sector from alumni. Downside is you do compete with international students heavily but I think they have capped international admittance a few years ago when this was exposed in their graduate program.
Edit: To give a bit more perspective, the program is a true computer science program with included concentrations.

bit more perspective, the program is a true computer science program with included concentrations. You can also explore their 4+1 program where your senior year you take graduate level courses which count credits towards both degrees. (Undergraduate + Graduate) The concentrations they offer are numerous as well compared to most NC schools.
Chapel Hill is a great brand name but not nearly as great or famous as alums think it is once u get outside of NC.
I'm sure UNCC's national brand is superior

famous as alums think it is once u get outside of NC.
I'm sure UNCC's national brand is superior
Never said it was. Sorry the truth about Chapel Hill hurts
Look at what schools have partnerships with as far as internships and other kinds of pipelines to employment. That's the only thing that matters in all honesty between the two.
I'm at UNC, but not a CS student. Did you get into the major or will you have to apply?

the two.
I'm at UNC, but not a CS student. Did you get into the major or will you have to apply? From what I know (I've only taken Comp110 so I'm not the best judge), the UNC program is a lot more theory-based than practical. It's also a PAIN to get into some of these classes here and they're always in a required sequence. However, the UNC name carries a lot more weight than you can imagine.

in a required sequence. However, the UNC name carries a lot more weight than you can imagine. Honestly I don't think you'll go wrong whether you choose UNC or UNCC in this case, because Charlotte's program is still very strong.
Also Chapel Hill specific, the town is (in my opinion) boring in comparison to Charlotte. Extremely hard to park anywhere. Extremely pricey housing, and even if you have roommates it's somehow more expensive than most locations in Charlotte.

and even if you have roommates it's somehow more expensive than most locations in Charlotte. I'd really consider these factors in your choice, because these issues definitely have pushed me to start a transfer application to UNCC in the past.
UnoriginalName432
Interesting, I hadn't considered that!
UnoriginalName432
I'm an incoming freshman and so I wouldn't know if i got into the major until spring 2027 because of how the application works.

so I wouldn't know if i got into the major until spring 2027 because of how the application works. Class registration is definitely a big consideration of mine
Thats probably working in your favor. I have a CS degree from UNCC. Now is not the best time to start a CS degree. In a 12-18 months one of two things will happen. 1. AI models will not improve beyond the current state(in terms of coding) 2. One of the AI labs will drastically improve the coding ability of it's model.

terms of coding) 2. One of the AI labs will drastically improve the coding ability of it's model. If 1 companies will be forced to hire SWEs for the foreseeable future, if 2 software engineering won't be a job anymore.
I think the lower level comp classes hold seats for freshmen, but it's still incredibly difficult to get in because so many people are taking them in preparation for applying for the major. You mentioned Honors college at UNCC, did you get into Honors Carolina?

for applying for the major. You mentioned Honors college at UNCC, did you get into Honors Carolina? That will be extremely helpful here. I transferred to UNC from Community College (so junior standing) and I only got into Comp110 because it was a late added section and it got filled incredibly fast (I was 20 on the waitlist). But honestly without the guaranteed major I'd be leaning toward UNCC, safer and still an amazing school for CS.

without the guaranteed major I'd be leaning toward UNCC, safer and still an amazing school for CS. Or explore other majors at UNC such as Data Science (also requires an application) or Stats. It's just rough out here 😭
Can't speak to Chapel Hill but Charlotte treated me well and made it extremely easy to get employment following graduation. Plus you have options like the early entry MS program and CyberCorps SFS which you should look into.

you have options like the early entry MS program and CyberCorps SFS which you should look into.
Computer Science and Cybersecurity are very different as majors, you should really explore that carefully.
That said, just in terms of the school alone, there's a case to be made for NCSU over UNC for CS ... I'm not so sure it applies with UNCC ...

there's a case to be made for NCSU over UNC for CS ... I'm not so sure it applies with UNCC ... but if your honors college admission comes with significant financial aid at UNCC that you won't be getting at UNC, I would consider that probably above all. If you can somehow make it out of UNCC without much in the way of student loans, but UNC will have you taking out a bunch of student loans, I would probably pick UNCC in that circumstance ... for CS

taking out a bunch of student loans, I would probably pick UNCC in that circumstance ... for CS
Cybersecurity is a concentration within the Computer Science major, they are not different majors, at least at Charlotte.
OK, yes, that is fair.
I should have perhaps used the word "disciplines" rather than "majors" in that case ... I meant in terms of possible career pathways/expectations
who's gonna tell him?
Go to chapel hill and never look back
Did my cs from uncc, wouldn't regret one bit.

gonna tell him?
Go to chapel hill and never look back
Did my cs from uncc, wouldn't regret one bit. Although i have to say there are some bad professors for sure. And professors who you'll have hard time understanding because of language barrier (chinese) and in the end job search is independent of the coursework. That is, your interview prep is different from class coursework. I went on to work for FAANG big tech in the valley rn.

prep is different from class coursework. I went on to work for FAANG big tech in the valley rn. I also love that with uncc you get a full campus experience and city life. Chapel hill idk didn't like the campus feel that much. Uncc campus is amazing and so vibrant with college life and activities to do
UnoriginalName432
I've heard about the bad professors. I'm hoping that the honors college priority registration would be able to offset that.

How is uncc?
Academic
Hey everyone I'm a senior in high school and plan to attend Charlotte! If anyone could let me know what it's really like that'd be awesome.
Parties
The people
The city life etc
I was a pretty dedicated athlete in high school and spent a lot of time doing my sport (I still had fun time to time) and I obviously want the college experience. I'm just curious on how uncc really is.
So I'm a transfer student from NC State originally who just came to Charlotte.

how uncc really is.
So I'm a transfer student from NC State originally who just came to Charlotte. I'm a commuter so I can't really speak for the parties. As for the people thus far, everybody is super kind and friendly. I never had any friends at NC State, but in this first week, I made several connections!
I've lived in Charlotte my entire life. There's always something to do. Just right off campus, there are multiple shopping centers, restaurants, etc.

something to do. Just right off campus, there are multiple shopping centers, restaurants, etc. there are things like top golf, concord mills (a nice mall in the area), and obviously the city itself and there are plenty of things to do (museums, concerts, bars, etc). As a UNCC student, you get access to the light rail for free, and UNCC has its own bus that takes students to shopping centers.
Academically, the teachers here are great.

has its own bus that takes students to shopping centers.
Academically, the teachers here are great. My teachers at NC State hardly taught and everything was self taught. My professors at Charlotte (as an engineering major) seem super passionate about what they teach and genuinely care about the success of their students.
From what I've seen, the dorms are nice and the dining options on campus are tons better. We have places like subway, Panda Express, bojangles, Wendy's, chick fil a, and more.

tons better. We have places like subway, Panda Express, bojangles, Wendy's, chick fil a, and more.
Campus in general is beautiful. I don't think I've ever seen buildings like the ones I have class in! Also since you're super athletic, I think you would like UREC. It's an amazing facility with lots of fitness options available. They also have some amazing recreational areas for general sports, so if you play tennis, you get access to the courts as well.

areas for general sports, so if you play tennis, you get access to the courts as well.
Lastly, there are many on-campus amenities and events. We have a movie theater, a lounge in the student union that has billiards and gaming stations, a botanical garden, a recreational outdoor pool, and more. Some events we do are Gold Rush which is the welcome week event, which has many many MANY opportunities to connect.
I promise you Charlotte was the greatest decision I've ever made. I love it here!

to connect.
I promise you Charlotte was the greatest decision I've ever made. I love it here! DM me if you have any questions.
I'm an engineering major as well and that's kind of why I chose Charlotte over state because I heard the same thing about the classes and engineering environment there.

over state because I heard the same thing about the classes and engineering environment there. It looks like a great school and the alumni network is a bit stronger but there's so many people trying to go there and i heard most of them spend more time on research than teaching although that's an issue at most universities.
My mom's colleague is an engineering mentor who works with students both from State and Charlotte.

mom's colleague is an engineering mentor who works with students both from State and Charlotte. He says the NC State students are subpar compared to UNCC students and the UNCC students are more knowledgeable.
I also notice a lot of the classes here are hands-on or interactive instead of just lecture which is something that helps me learn. I never had that at State.

instead of just lecture which is something that helps me learn. I never had that at State.
I did not like how everybody felt like they were trying too hard at state because it made me feel intimidated, but here the energy is more laid back which helps me tons because it makes me feel more comfortable.
Also, it seems like it may be easier to do research or do an internship here. I could go on and on. I love it here!
Organic-Pepper-434
I really appreciate the feedback!!!

here. I could go on and on. I love it here!
Organic-Pepper-434
I really appreciate the feedback!!!
I did 2 years at state in computer science. Worst decision ever. 0 help from professors, impossible to get help in office hours, constantly belittled by them, and found them very anti-military. Had a few friends commit suicide and know some who got cancer from Poe hall. I had the same issues with having to be almost completely self taught.

got cancer from Poe hall. I had the same issues with having to be almost completely self taught.
There's a million more career fairs and opportunities here compared to state since we're in Charlotte. I've been able to actually get campus jobs and get all the help I need to get a 4.0 gpa consistently. Had to retake some com sci classes here and the content is nearly identical (besides some programming classes). Professors seem to actually care about us in my experience.

(besides some programming classes). Professors seem to actually care about us in my experience.
I will say, I did have a better time with friends at NCSU and Raleigh is safer + cheaper ... but that's all. There are great departments with very good culture but the com sci department was terrible to me, and often found people leaving com sci for other engineering disciplines.
Everyone has their own unique experience, but I wouldn't recommend NCSU overall compared to Charlotte.

has their own unique experience, but I wouldn't recommend NCSU overall compared to Charlotte.
They are mostly there for research from what I've heard. My cousin who went to Georgia Tech said all colleges would be like that, but to be honest, I think it has to do with the fact these professors want such a prestigious school's name on their research, and also the grants and other perks associated with it.
I was so happy when I got to UNCC and found that ALL of my teachers teach.

associated with it.
I was so happy when I got to UNCC and found that ALL of my teachers teach. I remember reading some of the rate my professors for my professors and they literally said "professor teaches from a slide show" but I remember asking myself, "yall get slideshows? That is a privilege at NCSU". I would much rather teach from a slideshow than to have to teach myself.
I am so sorry to hear about what has happened to some of your friends!

to have to teach myself.
I am so sorry to hear about what has happened to some of your friends! They make it feel so impossible to graduate with high standing at NCSU.

Financial Aid
I'm in incoming transfer student, and didn't hear anything about any merit scholarships. I know I won't get anything from fafsa, but when i looked at the online uncc scholarship portal it didn't show up anything (and I assume it's because I don't have a uncc gpa yet) what have other people who needed aid done?

oak hall
Housing/Sublease
Hi!! basically the title, I got assigned to oak hall 2x4 suit and was just wondering if there's anything i should know about the hall. tysm:)

How is the social life at UNCC? Is it really dead on the weekends?
Academic
I am a senior currently applying to colleges, and UNCC is one of my top choices. Except, I haven't visited yet. I am out of state, (Ohio resident) so I am unfamiliar with the culture in NC. From the research I've done, the campus looks beautiful and seems great for what I want to major in, but my biggest concern is the social life. I keep hearing that its a BIG commuter campus. and practically dead on the weekends.

social life. I keep hearing that its a BIG commuter campus. and practically dead on the weekends. I'm not looking for a party school necessarily, but I still want a social life if I am going to be moving far away. So are the rumors true? How has the experience been for those not commuting?
Archived post. New comments cannot be posted and votes cannot be cast.

for those not commuting?
Archived post. New comments cannot be posted and votes cannot be cast.
It's 100% dead on the weekends but you would make friends that live on campus and you could see them on weekdays, that doesn't make it any harder to see people and make plans. My only question is why you'd pay out of state tuition to move from Ohio to Charlotte, North Carolina?
pandapersonUFO
Because I hate it here lol
Lmao Charlotte is basically Columbus but a little less cold.

I hate it here lol
Lmao Charlotte is basically Columbus but a little less cold. UNCC is a fine cheap paper but I would NEVER pay out of state to go here without like a full ride.
I am pro-Charlotte as much as anyone but to be perfectly honest, there really isn't much about this school that would make it worth going out of state for. Trust me, there are better institutions within Ohio that would most likely be cheaper with being in state.
Does it still get super dead during the holidays?

would most likely be cheaper with being in state.
Does it still get super dead during the holidays? I remember it being an absolute ghost town
It's a commuter campus so it's dead in terms of typical college life but if you can't find something to do in the city of Charlotte you're not looking hard enough
Personally, I always thought it being dead was an appeal. Should you want to fully make use of a campus's resources, then the weekend is the time to do so.

Should you want to fully make use of a campus's resources, then the weekend is the time to do so. Library, dining halls/other restaurants, gym, etc That may be the core few, but don't sleep on how much easier that makes life on campus. If you live on campus, it is especially easier since you can enjoy campus without the hustle and bustle. Likewise, you'll be able to more easily do laundry and, if you have roommates, there is a decent likelihood they will be among the few departing on weekends.

you have roommates, there is a decent likelihood they will be among the few departing on weekends. Don't forget, there is still a city and still people who stay on or near campus on the weekends. Not everyone wants to spend their free weekends with their family.
Yes, it is dead. I have come in on Saturdays and Sundays before and unless there are major events, it becomes a ghost town.
It's quiet on weekends, but not sure it's really a "commuter" campus in the strictest sense.

town.
It's quiet on weekends, but not sure it's really a "commuter" campus in the strictest sense. Huge population of students live "off-campus" but within a 5 min walk. Also a very urban campus, so as others have said, plenty to do around campus or grab Blue line from campus to Uptown.
The campus is beautiful and Charlotte is probably a better city than any city in Ohio except maybe Columbus.

is beautiful and Charlotte is probably a better city than any city in Ohio except maybe Columbus. What's Pine Hall Like?
Housing/Sublease
Hey! I am going to be a freshman in the fall and am wondering how pine hall was like for anyone who's stayed there before. I just got assigned it as my housing and am excited, but also want to know any tips, suggestions, pros, cons, etc, and if anyone has any pictures of what it looked like. Did you have enough space for your clothes, etc? Thanks!
A friend of mine stayed there my sophomore year so I went over there a lot to work on projects,

friend of mine stayed there my sophomore year so I went over there a lot to work on projects,
Compared to Belk or Martin which were the dorms I stayed in 3 of the 4 years, it's smaller, rooms just aren't as big or tall. The building layout was a little weird, with a central staircase per hallway with each half story landing having 2 rooms.

a little weird, with a central staircase per hallway with each half story landing having 2 rooms. One of the standout features of the building were most of the rooms having a balcony connected to the common living space which can be nicer in the non summer/mid winter months. I do remember my friend having some AC problems, I forget if the unit broke or if air movement was just slow and it got stuffy.
In terms of location on campus it's one of the better ones.

was just slow and it got stuffy.
In terms of location on campus it's one of the better ones. There's the student Union on one side, main general educational buildings across the street, and the science building on the other side of which has Auntie Anne's. Parking, per usual, isn't the best but there are parking spots right up to the buildings so if you do get a spot there then you don't have to walk far.

spots right up to the buildings so if you do get a spot there then you don't have to walk far. Overall I'd say the best thing going for Pine is its close location to the Union, parking if you can get it, and proximity to a lot of the education buildings. But for apartment style dorms I'd say it's on the lower end in terms of space.

It's been an ongoing joke for like the last 30 years that most undergraduates (Bachelor degrees) only require a 2.0 GPA (Grade Point Average - D = 1.0, C = 2.0, B = 3.0, A = 4.0) to graduate.

only require a 2.0 GPA (Grade Point Average - D = 1.0, C = 2.0, B = 3.0, A = 4.0) to graduate. It applies to most colleges/universities.
FOR EXAMPLE PURPOSES ONLY (Don't do this, always try your best): Assume all classes are 3 credit hours each. lets say you get 2 easy early classes for your major (1000 level) and make an A in both: you allow yourself the flexibility of 3 D's later on when you hit burnout phase during the harder (4000 level) classes.

flexibility of 3 D's later on when you hit burnout phase during the harder (4000 level) classes.
Charlotte is pretty bad if you aren't from another mid-big sized city i.e. Chicago, Milwaukee, Minneapolis, etc. Walking to places also takes some getting used to; you have to be patient. Pros: Dining hall food isn't unbearable Dorms are nice Campus is super nice Decent academics Diverse Cons: Lack of party life Boring on weekends All the stairs and hills
On Campus
UNCC is great for some people, but it is definitely not for everyone. If all you care about is how pretty the buildings and campus are then go for it. But if you want the "normal" college town vibe, this is not the place.

campus are then go for it. But if you want the "normal" college town vibe, this is not the place. Due to UNCC being newer than CLT itself the campus feels very out of place compared to the city off-campus. It almost feels like campus is a contained bubble that you do not want to venture off of. If you want a school with lower mid-tier sports and school spirit then this is the place. The academics are great, but the social scene is still in its infancy compared to other NC schools.

academics are great, but the social scene is still in its infancy compared to other NC schools.
Pros: UNCC has a good stem program, besides physics. The campus is almost completely self contained, meaning that the only people that are around campus are people on official business with the school... at least for the most part. I know that NC State for example has its campus spread across Raleigh, which has to suck for the students.

that NC State for example has its campus spread across Raleigh, which has to suck for the students. If you want to go to Charlotte downtown or wherever, there's a light rail on campus and it's free for students up to a certain point. Never took it myself, so can't say what point. I really like all of the professors that I've taken classes with the last few semesters, I'm in the math dept though, so I can't really speak to professors of most other colleges.

I'm in the math dept though, so I can't really speak to professors of most other colleges. UNCC has 7 colleges, each offering a lot of degrees. No worries, all 7 colleges are on the university campus, and typically you'll take classes from multiple colleges, but probably not from all of them. UNCC has a lot of dining options. They just built the UREC in 2019.

probably not from all of them. UNCC has a lot of dining options. They just built the UREC in 2019. It's a giant, 5 story gym, with an indoor running track, a massive pool, that is easily the size of 5 pools combined, if not more, indoor basketball courts, I think it has indoor tennis courts, a whole floor dedicated to cardio, weightlifting areas, etc., I think there are other pros that I'm not thinking of, but we'll leave it there.

areas, etc., I think there are other pros that I'm not thinking of, but we'll leave it there.
Cons: The crime in the area, while campus is generally safe, the area immediately around the campus is kind of sketchy, there isn't very many food options for those who have food allergies. I know a guy that was managing to go gluten free, I don't know how he found food to eat. Another con, the physics dept. is absolutely terrible. I'm sure there are also more cons that I'm not thinking of.

physics dept. is absolutely terrible. I'm sure there are also more cons that I'm not thinking of.
Anyway, point is that I really love this school, and wouldn't go anywhere else. However, it's not the school for everyone. Tour it before you decide to come here.

I would wager that, from their behavior, 90% of UNCC students have some form of Aspergers or ADHD. The lack of eye contact and basic social awareness at this school is something you will have to deal with for years if you choose to come here. The amount of people who don't exercise, are overweight, and just lack basic self awareness is quite noticeable, and this is not a post to be negative.

and just lack basic self awareness is quite noticeable, and this is not a post to be negative. this is a post to UNC Charlotte students and to me (I was a former student) that, if it wasn't obvious enough, please be self aware and care enough to change for the better. As someone with Aspergers and add, I used to have, and, to some extent, still have the same issues, but I was willing to change myself for the better. Everyone who wants to succeed in life must develop basic skills.

to change myself for the better. Everyone who wants to succeed in life must develop basic skills. Go to therapy, get medicated, eat a healthy diet, exercise, dress well, and make your bed in the morning - simple things like this make a difference. UNC Charlotte's food services is littered with fast food chains, which isn't helpful for the amount of obesity in the students population nor is it conducive to healthy cognitive function - I would suggest the school offer healthier options - sushi,

to healthy cognitive function - I would suggest the school offer healthier options - sushi, more vegetables, etc. This, by itself, would make a significant difference. Change isn't easy, but it is a must. All these things not only help yourself but make you a more pleasant person to be around.

a must. All these things not only help yourself but make you a more pleasant person to be around. Nobody wants to be around someone who doesn't shower, doesn't make eye contact, is overweight, refuses to change, constantly complains about everything, uses slang to communicate, and seems to lack self awareness - this is how UNC Charlotte students are right now, but they can change if they put in effort. Will they do it? That's up to them.

are right now, but they can change if they put in effort. Will they do it? That's up to them. This is why there is such a massive difference between UNC Charlotte and UNC Chapel Hill. UNC Chapel Hill students don't exhibit the behaviors I mentioned above(lacking basic social skills and self awareness, using slang to communicate). People treat you how you treat yourself. I know this is a long post, but I hope that someone will take this advice to better themselves.

I know this is a long post, but I hope that someone will take this advice to better themselves.

life, but you need to go out of your way more for it I'd say. Go make friends and go to events. Parties aren't HUGE but it's not like there isn't any. There's so much to do in Charlotte that most just go out in the city which imo is better than parties.
